steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage states that the mosque “combines elements from the Persian, Mughal, and Moorish styles”—this decisive phrase shows multiple distinct architectural traditions being brought together.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The blank needs to name the result of combining multiple architectural styles — the Al Noor Mosque blends Persian, Mughal, and Moorish elements. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — fusion: "a unique fusion of those influences" — fusion means the blending of distinct elements into a unified whole, exactly what combining three architectural styles produces. ✓
  • Choice B — dismissal: "a unique dismissal of those influences" — dismissal means rejection; a mosque that combines styles does not reject them. ✗
  • Choice C — critique: "a unique critique of those influences" — critique means a critical analysis or judgment, not a physical blend of styles. ✗
  • Choice D — simplification: "a unique simplification of those influences" — simplification means reducing complexity; merging three styles is not a reduction. ✗
That is the correct answer.
